sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: UP didn't change the main game objects (ids 11, 12, 13 stayed the same), but the board did change. Let me check what actually changed - maybe it's the selector bar or some other element. Let me compare before and after more carefully, and also try DOWN and SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed with UP - compare full segmentation
before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after_seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("Comparing all nodes:")
before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}

for nid in sorted(set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if bn and an:
        changed = bn['hash'] != an['hash'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']
        if changed:
            print(f"  id={nid} CHANGED: before hash={bn['hash']} pix={bn['pixels']} -> after hash={an['hash']} pix={an['pixels']}")
    elif bn:
        print(f"  id={nid} REMOVED")
    else:
        print(f"  id={nid} ADDED")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
